behaviors.description: The cron parser rejects invalid expressions (e.g., month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with a descriptive error message.
behaviors.justification: Pure parsing and validation logic with no external dependencies, I/O, or LLM involvement. Validation rules are deterministic and can be tested with direct function calls.
behaviors.test_description: Call parser.parse('13 * * * *') and verify ValidationError raised with message indicating invalid month (0-11 or 1-12 depending on convention). Call parser.parse('* * 32 * *') and verify error for day out of range. Call parser.parse('abc def ghi jkl') and verify error for malformed syntax. Test null/empty string input. Verify each error message specifically names the failing field and the constraint violated.

behaviors.description: The next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including edge cases like 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday.'
behaviors.justification: Pure calculation logic with no I/O or external dependencies. All edge cases (leap years, month boundaries, weekday arithmetic) can be tested deterministically with explicit inputs and verified against known correct outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Test cron '0 9 * * 1-5' from 2025-03-01 12:00 returns 2025-03-03 09:00 (next Monday). Test 'last day of month' cron (e.g., '0 0 L * *') for Feb in leap (2024) and non-leap (2025) years returns 28th and 29th respectively. Test 'nearest weekday' cron (e.g., '0 9 15W * *') for the 15th falling on weekend returns the nearest weekday. Test month boundaries (e.g., April 31st doesn't exist) wraps to next month. Test multiple run times in one day are returned in order.

behaviors.description: The overlap detector identifies when two schedules would fire within the configured minimum gap (e.g., 5 minutes) and reports the conflicting time windows.
behaviors.justification: Comparison and windowing logic operating on schedule objects with no external dependencies or I/O. Overlaps are computed deterministically from cron expressions and run-time calculations.
behaviors.test_description: Create Schedule S1 with cron '0 9 * * 1' (Monday 9:00 AM) and S2 with cron '0 9:05 * * 1' (Monday 9:05 AM). Call detector.find_overlaps(S1, S2, min_gap_minutes=10) and verify returns conflict window [09:00-09:05] on Mondays. Create non-overlapping schedules (S1 Monday 9 AM, S2 Tuesday 9 AM) and verify no overlap returned. Test boundary: S1 at 9:00, S2 at 9:10 with min_gap=10 should return no overlap (separated by exactly the gap). Test S1 and S2 firing at the exact same time returns conflict. Test multiple conflicts across different days.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling produces correct run times across a DST transition — a job scheduled for 2:30 AM handles the spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity correctly.
behaviors.justification: Pure calculation using real timezone objects (zoneinfo.ZoneInfo); DST transitions are deterministic mathematical facts with known dates and can be tested without changing system timezone or observing actual job execution. The test uses real timezone data but not system-level intervention.
behaviors.test_description: For cron '30 2 * * *' in America/New_York timezone, starting 2025-03-08 01:00 EST, verify next run is 2025-03-09 03:30 EDT (spring-forward: 2:00-3:00 AM doesn't exist on March 9, so 2:30 AM is adjusted or skipped per implementation policy). For cron '30 1 * * *' in America/New_York on 2025-11-02 (fall-back when 1:00-2:00 AM occurs twice), verify the calculator consistently picks first or second occurrence per documented policy. Test that a job scheduled for a non-existent time (like 2:30 AM during spring-forward) is handled without throwing an exception. Test multiple timezones with different DST rules (e.g., UTC+0, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo).

behaviors.description: When a scheduled job's computed next run time has already passed (e.g., system was down), the engine determines whether to skip or backfill based on the job's configured missed-run policy.
behaviors.justification: Policy decision logic that is deterministic given inputs (current time, job config, policy setting). No I/O, external services, or actual job execution needed; decision is made through pure conditional logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create job with next scheduled run 2025-03-28 09:00 and current time 2025-03-28 10:30. With policy SKIP, verify handle_missed_run(job, current_time) returns SKIP action with no backfill queued. With policy BACKFILL_RECENT, verify returns BACKFILL for the one missed run at 09:00. With policy BACKFILL_ALL and job last ran 2025-03-27 09:00, verify returns ALL missed runs (2025-03-27 09:00, 2025-03-28 09:00). Test boundary: current time exactly equal to next run time is not considered missed. Test with a job that runs every 5 minutes and has missed 10 runs; verify BACKFILL_ALL queues all 10, BACKFILL_RECENT queues only the most recent, SKIP queues none.
